Title: NAKED ENDOSPERM1, NAKED ENDOSPERM2, and OPAQUE2 interact to regulate gene networks in maize endosperm development

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: endosperm, especially starchy endosperm and aleurone/subaleurone compartments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed nutrient storage for grain filling, germination, and seedling establishment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation and repression depending on target; chromatin-accessibility modulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native endogenous promoter (mutant analysis in W22); no transgenic promoter used
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm during 8–16 DAP, acting during transition from cellular development to grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutation causes reduced starch and storage protein accumulation, defective aleurone/subaleurone differentiation, increased endoreduplication, wrinkled/shrunken kernels in combinations
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage (8, 12, 16 DAP) during grain filling transition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single/double/triple mutant analysis; RNA-seq; WGCNA; DAP-seq; ATAC-seq; starch quantification; HPLC zein profiling; microscopy
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental program of endosperm; feedback from NKD2 and O2
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms homo/heterodimers with NKD proteins; physically interacts with IDDP10
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in feedback with NKD2; NKD2 may repress nkd1, helping limit NKD levels
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates o2; represses some developmental genes such as MYBR24-associated module genes; promotes accessibility of O2 target sites
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central regulator coordinating transition from cellular development to nutrient accumulation and contributing to starch/protein/lipid storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes starch accumulation and storage protein accumulation, restricts excess cellular development and endoreduplication in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: grain filling; starch metabolism; storage protein accumulation; lipid storage; developmental constraint of auxin/cell wall programs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target for improving endosperm filling and storage quality, but perturbation likely causes severe kernel development defects so careful stacking/editing would be required
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NKD1 has fewer DAP-seq peaks than NKD2; all NKD1-bound genes were contained among NKD2-bound genes; nkd1 shows stronger synthetic interaction with o2 than nkd2 does
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NKD1 is a central IDD transcription factor required for proper maize endosperm development during the 8–16 DAP transition. 2. Loss of NKD1 contributes to reduced starch and zein accumulation and interacts synthetically with o2 and nkd2 to worsen grain-filling defects. 3. NKD1 cooperates with NKD2 and O2 in a dynamic hierarchical network that restricts developmental programs while promoting storage metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ATAC-seq showed NKD1 influences chromatin accessibility and that O2 affects accessibility of NKD target sites.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: endosperm, especially starchy endosperm and aleurone/subaleurone compartments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed nutrient storage for grain filling, germination, and seedling establishment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation and repression; dynamic coregulation with NKD1 and O2; increases O2 target-site accessibility
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native endogenous promoter (mutant analysis in W22); no transgenic promoter used
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutation with nkd1 causes severe aleurone defects, reduced starch, altered starch granules, reduced storage proteins, increased endoreduplication
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage (8, 12, 16 DAP) during grain filling transition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single/double/triple mutant analysis; RNA-seq; WGCNA; DAP-seq; ATAC-seq; starch quantification; HPLC zein profiling; microscopy
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental program; O2 positively regulates nkd2; feedback with NKD1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: o2; ARF1; ARF29; PLATZ2; GRFTF3; GAP1; GLK11; ABI39; HB75; MYBR24
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: can form homodimers or heterodimers with NKD1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in mutual regulation with nkd1; O2 transactivates nkd2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates o2; may repress arf1, hb75, and mybr24; regulates auxin, cell wall, lipid, starch, and nutrient reservoir modules
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major hierarchical regulator linking developmental patterning to storage metabolism; strongest direct-target evidence among the three via DAP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes starch, storage protein, and lipid accumulation while restricting inappropriate developmental programs and endoreduplication
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: grain filling; starch biosynthesis; nutrient reservoir activity; lipid biosynthesis/storage; hormone-linked developmental control
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: key engineering node for endosperm filling, but broad developmental effects mean dosage/context must be controlled
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NKD2 had many more DAP-seq targets than NKD1 and directly targets o2 as a hub gene for nutrient storage modules; enriched direct-target GO terms include starch biosynthesis and nutrient reservoir activity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NKD2 is a major IDD transcription factor controlling maize endosperm transition to grain filling. 2. NKD2 directly targets many regulatory genes and o2, acting hierarchically to control starch, storage protein, lipid, hormone, and cell wall networks. 3. NKD2 collaborates with NKD1 and O2 to promote nutrient accumulation and to shape chromatin accessibility.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ATAC-seq showed NKD2 affects chromatin accessibility at target loci; O2 strongly influences accessibility of NKD2 target sites.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: endosperm, mainly starchy endosperm protein bodies
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage proteins support grain filling and seedling nutrition; lysine is an essential amino acid for nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; hierarchical network regulation; global chromatin landscape modulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native endogenous promoter (mutant analysis in W22); no transgenic promoter used
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm, especially influential at 12 and 16 DAP during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: o2 mutation reduces zein accumulation and starch content, causes opaque kernels, alters protein body morphology, but increases lysine content
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage (8, 12, 16 DAP) during endosperm grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single/double/triple mutant analysis; RNA-seq; integration with published ChIP-seq targets; WGCNA; ATAC-seq; HPLC zein profiling; starch assay; microscopy
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: NKD1 and NKD2 positively regulate o2; developmental grain-filling cues
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: zein storage protein genes; bzip17; gbf1; nkd2; floury2; tryptophan aminotransferase related3
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NKD2; PBF1; OHP1; OHP2; ZmbZIP22; ZmMADS47
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms protein complexes with PBF1, OHP1, OHP2, ZmbZIP22, and ZmMADS47
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of dynamic circuit where O2 activates nkd2 and is positively regulated by NKD1/NKD2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates zein genes and hub TFs bzip17 and gbf1; promotes nutrient reservoir modules
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key regulator of grain filling and storage protein biosynthesis; strongest genotype effect on transcriptome at 12–16 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes zein accumulation and starch deposition; loss causes opaque kernels with reduced storage proteins and increased lysine
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage protein biosynthesis; starch biosynthesis; nutrient metabolism; grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: classical nutritional quality target because o2 elevates lysine, but must be balanced against severe kernel texture and storage defects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: O2 influences the global regulatory landscape, decreasing accessibility of NKD target sites when mutated; o2-containing mutant combinations show especially strong storage-protein defects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE174059); ATAC-seq (GSE214415); published O2 ChIP-seq integrated in this study; DAP-seq dataset GSE214166 for downstream TFs/NKD factors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 is a central bZIP regulator of maize endosperm grain filling, especially storage protein accumulation. 2. O2 works with NKD1 and NKD2 in a dynamic hierarchical network that promotes starch, protein, and lipid storage while restraining inappropriate developmental programs. 3. O2 also shapes the global chromatin accessibility landscape and its mutation strongly perturbs endosperm transcriptomes and nutrient storage.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ATAC-seq showed O2 broadly influences chromatin accessibility, including NKD1/NKD2 target-site accessibility.
